beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, check calendar and emails.
beats.reasoning: River starts the week with full energy; morning routine is efficient and deliberate, befitting their organized nature.

beats.activity: Commute to Redmond workplace via car, avoiding Light Rail closure.
beats.reasoning: Light Rail is closed 9am–3pm; River drives to Redmond, arriving before the closure window affects any transit. Early start ensures smooth commute.

beats.activity: Speech therapy sessions with pediatric clients, documentation, and team check-in.
beats.reasoning: River's primary work block before lunch; four hours of focused client sessions and admin work fits a typical Monday morning in her role.

beats.activity: Lunch break at workplace café, light meal and brief rest.
beats.reasoning: River takes a quiet lunch at work rather than venturing out; she's mindful of expense tracking and prefers familiar, controlled environments during the workday.

beats.activity: Afternoon speech therapy sessions, progress notes, and staff meeting.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon client block and administrative duties; River's work rhythm includes back-to-back sessions with brief documentation windows.

beats.activity: Commute home to Seattle via car, avoiding Light Rail closure impact.
beats.reasoning: Light Rail closure still in effect until 3pm; River leaves work and drives home, arriving in Rainier Valley by early evening.

beats.activity: Quick dinner prep and eat at home; review vinyl collection and relax.
beats.reasoning: River keeps dinner simple on a work day, then unwinds with her vinyl collecting hobby—a low-key, cost-conscious evening that suits her personality.

beats.activity: Commute to Capitol Hill for evening social plans.
beats.reasoning: River values time with close friends; she heads to Capitol Hill for a casual evening out, taking advantage of the sunny weather and her full Monday energy.

beats.activity: Meet a close friend for drinks and conversation at a neighborhood bar; discuss work, climbing plans, and life.
beats.reasoning: River prioritizes meaningful connection over crowds; she spends the evening with a trusted friend in a familiar Capitol Hill venue, energized by genuine social time.

beats.activity: Commute back home to Rainier Valley.
beats.reasoning: River heads home after a pleasant evening; she's intentional about wrapping up social time and getting adequate rest before the work week.

beats.activity: Evening wind-down: skincare routine, review tomorrow's schedule, read briefly.
beats.reasoning: River's meticulous nature extends to evening rituals; she prepares for sleep and ensures tomorrow is organized, a calming routine that suits her personality.
River gets a full 7 hours 15 minutes of sleep, allowing recovery for a busy work week; sleep ends well before dawn of the next day.

diary:
Monday came in bright and warm. I skipped the Light Rail mess and drove out to Redmond early, grateful for the sunshine on the highway. The kids were chatty today—one of my seven-year-olds finally nailed that /r/ sound we've been working on for weeks. Small victories feel huge in this work.

Lunch was quiet in the café, just me and my thoughts. I caught myself thinking about the World Cup match everyone was talking about, all those bars downtown probably packed by now. Not my scene anymore, but there's something nice about a whole city buzzing with the same energy.

After work I headed to Capitol Hill to meet Marcus. We grabbed drinks at Kremwerk and talked climbing, work stress, the usual. The evening had that golden-hour glow, and for a while I forgot about the drive times and the schedules. Just good conversation and cold beer. Drove home feeling settled.
thought_process: I made the practical choice this morning to drive instead of dealing with the Light Rail closure—no point fighting transit delays when I've got clients depending on me. The day felt routine but satisfying; I'm in a rhythm with my pediatric clients now, and that progress note I wrote for the seven-year-old was genuinely rewarding. I noticed the World Cup buzz around the office but didn't feel pulled toward it—I'm past the age of bar-hopping for sports, though I appreciated the collective excitement. My mood lifted considerably once I committed to seeing Marcus; after a solid but contained workday, I needed that social anchor. The drive home was peaceful, and I'm feeling the kind of tired that comes from a full, purposeful day.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
